metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the false premise: `adf.py` is dynamically loaded via `lazy_load` in at least three callers (`outbound_fields.py:69`, `outbound_comments.py:84`, `inbound_translate.py:293`); the plan must either exclude `adf.py` from Phase 1 relocation or add an explicit step to update all `lazy_load('...', 'adf.py')` call sites to use the new sub-package path.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'The Jira vendor modules (`acli*`, `adf.py`, `jira_fields.py`, `outbound_fields.py`) are imported normally (not dynamically loaded), so relocating them cannot break the loader.' This is factually false: `adf.py` IS dynamically loaded via `lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py')` inside `outbound_fields.py` (line 69), `outbound_comments.py` (line 84), and `inbound_translate.py` (line 293) — all `_PACKAGE_DIR`-relative. Moving `adf.py` to `adapters/jira/` without updating these callers WILL break the loader for those modules.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation section, point 2: 'The Jira vendor modules (acli*, adf.py, jira_fields.py, outbound_fields.py) are imported normally (not dynamically loaded), so relocating them cannot break the loader.'
findings.suggested_fix: In the 'Plan-review remediation' section, change the list of 'normally imported' vendor modules to remove `adf.py` (and verify `jira_fields.py` and `outbound_fields.py` similarly). For any module that IS dynamically loaded, either keep it at the package root in Phase 1 or add an explicit caller-update step to the Phase 1 AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's premise that adf.py is 'not dynamically loaded': audit all lazy_load and hardcoded-path callers for adf.py (outbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py, inbound_translate.py, reconcile_check.py, inbound_differ.py) and either (a) keep adf.py at root in Phase 1 and defer its move to Phase 2 alongside loader updates, or (b) update all caller load paths and _loader.py's _PACKAGE_DIR logic to support sub-directory resolution before moving adf.py.
findings.finding: The plan's premise that 'adf.py is imported normally (not dynamically loaded)' is factually incorrect: outbound_fields.py, outbound_comments.py, inbound_translate.py, and reconcile_check.py all load adf.py via lazy_load(_ADF_KEY, 'adf.py'), which resolves the path through _loader.py's _PACKAGE_DIR. If adf.py moves to adapters/jira/ but _loader.py stays at root, _PACKAGE_DIR still points to the package root and lazy_load('x', 'adf.py') will fail with ImportError because the file no longer exists at root. Additionally, inbound_differ.py uses a direct Path(__file__).parent / 'adf.py' hardcoded path (not through lazy_load) that will also break. This means moving adf.py breaks multiple callers through two distinct loading mechanisms — neither of which the plan identifies as requiring loader updates.
findings.location: Plan section 'Plan-review remediation': 'The Jira vendor modules (acli*, adf.py, jira_fields.py, outbound_fields.py) are imported normally (not dynamically loaded), so relocating them cannot break the loader.'
findings.suggested_fix: Keep adf.py at the package root in Phase 1 alongside the other dynamically-loaded modules; add it to the Phase 2 scope in the ADR with a note that it requires updating _loader.py's path resolution or converting all callers to a new sub-directory-aware lazy_load signature.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` and `src/rebar/_engine/jira-capability-probe.py` to the out-of-dir consumer enumeration — both import `acli` and/or `acli_subprocess` directly and must be updated or explicitly deferred if those modules relocate.
findings.finding: `scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py` and `src/rebar/_engine/jira-capability-probe.py` are out-of-reconciler-dir callers that import vendor modules (`acli_subprocess`, `acli`) directly via `from rebar_reconciler import ...`. These consumers are not enumerated in the plan's scope, and relocating the named vendor modules would require updating their imports.
findings.location: Plan section 'Phase 1 (this change)' / AC2; unenumerated consumers: scripts/capture_jira_fixtures.py:158-161; src/rebar/_engine/jira-capability-probe.py:32

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the enumeration of `lazy_load`-resolved modules: add `manifest_renderer.py` (loaded via `apply_planning.py`) and `comment_limits.py` (loaded via `outbound_comments.py`) to the list of files whose relocation requires updating loader call sites.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'It [the loader] loads only backend-neutral machinery (`mode.py`, `_advisory_lock.py`, `_ref_lock.py`, `mutation.py`, `_errors.py`)' as justification for why vendor-module relocation cannot break the loader. This is incomplete: `lazy_load` is also invoked with `'manifest_renderer.py'` (in `apply_planning.py` line 42) and `'comment_limits.py'` (in `outbound_comments.py` line 100), which are omitted from the plan's enumeration of dynamically-loaded modules.
findings.location: Plan-review remediation section, point 2: 'It loads only backend-neutral machinery (mode.py, _advisory_lock.py, _ref_lock.py, mutation.py, _errors.py)'
findings.suggested_fix: Expand the enumeration to: 'mode.py, _advisory_lock.py, _ref_lock.py, mutation.py, _errors.py, adf.py (3 callers), manifest_renderer.py, comment_limits.py'. The ADR must treat all of these as requiring loader-path updates before relocation.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate and update the 7+ test files that hardcode `SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py"` via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` — these are unenumerated out-of-dir consumers that break if `adf.py` relocates, and must be included in the Phase 1 migration-safety assessment.
findings.finding: Multiple test files outside `rebar_reconciler/` hardcode the physical path to `adf.py` via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("rebar_reconciler.adf", SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler" / "adf.py")`. If `adf.py` moves to `adapters/jira/` in Phase 1, these 7+ test files break, but the plan does not enumerate them as consumers requiring update.
findings.location: Plan section 'Phase 1 (this change)' / AC2; consumers: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/test_transition_error_soft_fail.py:44,52; test_applier_comment_error_surfaced.py:41,49; test_applier_stale_binding_404.py:53,61; test_applier_assignee_soft_fail.py:53,61; test_applier_create_comment_error_surfaced.py:41,49; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/conflict/test_status_transition_rest.py:38,46; tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/mutate/test_assignee_validation.py:24,32; tests/integration/rebar_reconciler/test_reconcile_roundtrip.py:59
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's consumer enumeration: any module whose relocation requires updating hardcoded `Path / "rebar_reconciler" / "<module>.py"` literals in tests constitutes an additional class of reference beyond `import`/`patch()` targets, and the ADR inventory should flag each vendor module's membership in this class.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reword AC2 'import paths preserved' to 'all references updated to new canonical paths; no broken imports' to match the remediation section's explicit no-shim, migrate-all-refs strategy.
findings.finding: AC2 states 'import paths preserved' but the remediation section explicitly says all references are migrated to the new canonical path with no shims — meaning old import paths are intentionally retired, not preserved. This is a terminological contradiction within the plan that could mislead an implementer into adding re-export shims to satisfy AC2.
findings.location: AC2 ('import paths preserved') vs 'Plan-review remediation' section ('ALL references are migrated to the new canonical path')
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'with import paths preserved' with 'with all internal, test, and dynamic references migrated to new canonical paths (full suite green)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the 10 test files that synthesize `rebar_reconciler.__path__` pointing only to the flat reconciler root — verify that none of the vendor modules relocated in Phase 1 are transitively imported inside those test contexts, or update the `__path__` seeding to include `adapters/jira/`.
findings.finding: At least 10 test files outside `rebar_reconciler/` seed `rebar_reconciler.__path__ = [str(SCRIPTS_DIR / "rebar_reconciler")]` to simulate package context. When `adapters/jira/` is created as a real sub-package under `rebar_reconciler/`, these seedings may be insufficient (the synthetic package object won't enumerate the new sub-directory), potentially breaking any test that imports from `rebar_reconciler.adapters.jira.*` in that patched context. The plan does not enumerate this consumer class.
findings.location: Plan section 'Phase 1 (this change)' / AC2; consumers: tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/apply/ (10 files seeding `_dr.__path__`)

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the minimum form of the 'second-backend interface sketch' (e.g. 'a Python `Protocol` or `ABC` stub with typed method signatures for issue CRUD, field mapping, and transport operations') so the criterion is objectively evaluable.
findings.finding: 'Second-backend interface sketch' in AC1 is a subjective qualifier — 'sketch' has no stated minimum fidelity (e.g. abstract base class vs docstring vs prose paragraph), making the criterion partially effort-framed and not fully measurable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'second-backend interface sketch' with 'a Python `Protocol` or abstract base class stub with typed method signatures covering at minimum: create/update/close issue, map outbound fields, and execute transport — checked in alongside the ADR'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the module count from 55 to 58 (or current actual count) throughout the plan and ensure the ADR inventory is built from a live `ls src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/*.py | wc -l` enumeration rather than the stated figure.
findings.finding: The plan asserts '55 flat .py files' and '55-module reconciler' but the actual directory listing of `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/` contains 58 `.py` files. The ADR's inventory, the Phase 1/Phase 2 split scope, and any size-gate updates all depend on an accurate module count.
findings.location: Why section: 'S5 — 55 flat .py files, no sub-packaging'; What section: '55-module reconciler is a flat namespace'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '55 flat .py files' with '58 flat .py files' (verify again at ADR-authoring time with a fresh count); add a note that the ADR must enumerate all files explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the ADR's file path (e.g. `docs/adr/NNNN-reconciler-vendor-seam.md`) and add a structural proving check for AC1/AC4 (e.g. 'file exists and contains H2 sections: Jira-Coupling Inventory, Vendor-Neutral Operations, Target Sub-Package Layout, Second-Backend Interface, How to Add a Backend').
findings.finding: The ADR acceptance criteria (AC1 and AC4) lack a concrete proving command and do not state the ADR's file path, making it impossible for an agent to verify completeness deterministically. 'ADR authored' with a prose content list is effort-framed, not observable.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC1: 'File created at `docs/adr/NNNN-reconciler-vendor-seam.md`; contains sections: ## Jira-Coupling Inventory, ## Vendor-Neutral Operations, ## Target Sub-Package Layout, ## Second-Backend Interface. Verifiable via: `grep -c "^## " docs/adr/NNNN-reconciler-vendor-seam.md` returns ≥ 4.' Move AC4 content into a required sub-section of the same ADR file.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the 'ADR decides the seam' constraint with the pre-decided `adapters/jira/` layout: either reframe the ADR as *documenting* the already-decided seam (not deciding it), or acknowledge the layout is provisional pending ADR ratification.
findings.finding: The 'What' section declares 'the ADR decides the seam' as an explicit constraint against pre-deciding the layout, yet the plan body (remediation section and ACs) pre-decides the exact seam as `adapters/jira/` with specific module assignments before the ADR is authored. An implementer following 'the ADR decides the seam' would wait for the ADR before committing to a layout; an implementer following the ACs would create `adapters/jira/` immediately — two contradictory directions.
findings.location: 'What' section ('the ADR decides the seam') vs 'Phase 1' and AC2 ('adapters/jira/ vendor package established as the seam')
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'the ADR decides the seam' to 'the ADR documents and ratifies the seam decided here' to make clear the ADR is a design record, not a gate on the layout choice.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the ADR file path (e.g., `docs/adr/NNNN-vendor-adapter-seam.md`) in the plan, and add an AC item to audit and update any existing developer docs (CLAUDE.md, READMEs) that describe the current flat reconciler namespace so they reflect the new sub-package layout after Phase 1.
findings.finding: The plan mandates a new ADR describing the target sub-package layout and vendor-adapter seam, but does not specify (a) where the ADR file will live in the repository, nor (b) whether any existing developer-navigation documents (e.g., CLAUDE.md, architecture READMEs, or contributor guides) that describe the current flat 55-module reconciler namespace will be updated or cross-referenced — leaving those potentially stale after Phase 1 relocates modules.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC: 'ADR committed to `docs/adr/NNNN-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md`; any existing CLAUDE.md / architecture README sections describing the flat reconciler namespace updated or cross-referenced to the ADR.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale in the 'What' or 'Phase 1' section explaining why partial physical relocation is included in Phase 1 alongside the ADR (e.g., to instantiate the seam as a concrete package boundary rather than a purely documentary one), so the implementer has a scope-decision principle.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for why Phase 1 includes physical file relocation (creating adapters/jira/ and moving modules) rather than authoring the ADR alone and deferring all relocation to a Phase 2 ticket. The plan mandates sequencing the code move after prior tickets and explicitly acknowledges the broad reference-migration surface that makes Phase 1 moves risky — yet it does not explain why partial relocation in the same change as the ADR is preferable to treating relocation as entirely Phase 2 scope.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that AC1's 'second-backend interface sketch' and AC4's documentation requirement serve a current done-definition (a committed second-backend initiative); if not, replace with 'document the extension point as a placeholder / open question in the ADR, to be refined when the second backend is scoped' to avoid YAGNI-violating premature interface design.
findings.finding: AC1 requires 'the interface a second backend would implement' as a deliverable of the ADR, and AC4 formalizes 'implement `adapters/<name>/` against the seam' as a done criterion — but no second backend exists, is committed, or has a current call-site anywhere in the codebase. Designing even a sketch interface for a hypothetical consumer violates YAGNI under Rule-of-Three (zero call-sites for the interface, no named second backend in-flight).
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 ('second-backend interface sketch') and AC4 ('Adding a second backend is documented in the ADR as...')
findings.suggested_fix: Narrow AC1 to: 'ADR authored: Jira-coupling inventory + vendor-neutral operation list + target sub-package layout + extension-point placeholder (second-backend interface to be designed when a concrete second backend is scoped).' Drop AC4 or reword it to: 'The ADR records the intended extension pattern (`adapters/<name>/`) without mandating a complete interface sketch.' This preserves the structural seam value while avoiding designing for a hypothetical consumer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the Phase 1 module relocation (e.g. 'revert the migration commit; the suite-green gate is the primary safeguard') or describe the expand-contract steps that bound the risk window.
findings.finding: The plan relocates modules and retires old import paths without stating an explicit rollback or back-out plan. T4 requires that a destructive/irreversible step include an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequencing — acknowledgement of the break alone is insufficient.
findings.location: 'Plan-review remediation — preservation mechanism + phased execution' section, Phase 1 description
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to Phase 1: 'Back-out: the migration is a single atomic commit; if a post-merge regression is found, revert the commit. The full-suite-green gate before merge is the primary safeguard against missed references.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate the specific modules that MUST be in `adapters/jira/` for Phase 1 to be complete (e.g. state 'at minimum: `acli_runner.py`, `adf.py`, `jira_fields.py`; `outbound_fields.py` is Phase 1 if its patch surface resolves cleanly, else Phase 2') so AC2 has a fixed, verifiable target.
findings.finding: 'The safe subset of vendor modules relocated there' in AC2 does not enumerate which modules constitute the safe subset, requiring the executor to independently judge safety — a real decision deferred to the executor.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2; Plan-review remediation section

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
